title: CIRCIA Report-Content Template Validator — every submission includes the 11 NPRM §226.8 required elements before transmission
exclusions: Excludes ransom-payment-only reports (those follow a different content template tracked under UC-22.54.3). Excludes pre-NPRM voluntary reports submitted with an interim template (those are tracked under UC-22.54.19). Excludes supplemental reports whose content is derivative of the original (each supplement has its own validation under UC-22.54.4).
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.54.12` archived per-submission to `audit_evidence` with `circia_clause="CIRCIA-NPRM-report-content"`. Every row pins (submission_draft_id, incident_id, element_a_entity_identification, element_b_incident_description, element_c_detection_determination_dates, element_d_affected_systems, element_e_impact, element_f_vulnerabilities_exploited, element_g_threat_actor, element_h_security_defences, element_i_mitigation_recovery, element_j_point_of_contact, element_k_ransom_payment_details, element_total_present, content_status, validator_signed_by). Dashboard `CIRCIA — Report Content Validator` shows draft submissions against the 11-element checklist.

compliance.assurance_rationale: The NPRM § 226.8 lists 11 required content elements: (a) entity identification, (b) incident description, (c) detection/determination dates, (d) affected systems, (e) impact, (f) vulnerabilities, (g) threat actor, (h) security defences, (i) mitigation/recovery, (j) point of contact, (k) ransom payment details. A submission missing any element risks a CISA RFI under § 681b(h). UC-22.54.12 validates the draft submission against this checklist before transmission. Marked 'full' because the UC operationalises the NPRM content requirement directly.
compliance.controlObjective: Proves every CIRCIA submission carries the 11 NPRM § 226.8 required content elements before transmission to the CISA Services Portal.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search `UC-22.54.12` archived to `audit_evidence`, the `CIRCIA — Report Content Validator` dashboard, and the validator-signed checklist for each submission.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Generate a draft submission missing `element_h_security_defences` and `element_g_threat_actor`. Before transmission, UC-22.54.12 MUST flag the draft with `content_status="incomplete"` and `element_total_present=9` (out of 11), and MUST block UC-22.54.2 transmission.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Generate a draft with all 11 elements populated; UC-22.54.12 MUST record `content_status="complete"` and clear the submission for transmission.

dataSources: CIRCIA submission drafts stored in `circia_submission_draft_lookup` with one column per required element (bool). SOAR `circia-portal-submit` playbook prefills the draft from SIR; UC-22.54.12 validates before submission. Cross-reference to `circia_reporting_status_lookup` for incident context.
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk SOAR (Splunkbase 4413), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621).
description: Validates every CIRCIA submission draft against the NPRM § 226.8 11-element checklist. Surfaces incomplete drafts before they reach the CISA Services Portal so the IR Commander or General Counsel can complete them.
value: An incomplete CIRCIA report is the most common cause of CISA RFI under § 681b(h). The 11 elements are concrete and testable; without a validator, the draft submission relies on the IR Commander's memory of the NPRM requirements. This UC instruments the checklist so the General Counsel can ship complete reports the first time.
implementation: (1) Extend the SOAR `circia-portal-submit` playbook to write a draft row to `circia_submission_draft_lookup` for review before transmission. (2) The IR Commander or General Counsel reviews the draft, completes any missing elements, and signs. (3) Schedule UC-22.54.12 every 15 minutes; route `content_status=incomplete` for drafts pending review. (4) The SOAR `circia-portal-transmit` step (separate from the prefill step) only fires after the draft is `complete`.
visualization: (Panel 1) Draft-status table: every draft submission with the 11-element checkbox and missing-elements list. (Panel 2) Completion rate trend: percentage of drafts complete on first review vs after revision. (Panel 3) Top missing-element kinds. (Panel 4) Draft-to-transmission latency: how long from draft creation to transmission.

references.title: CISA NPRM — Required Report Content (89 FR 23644 § 226.8)

knownFalsePositives:
1. **Unknown threat actor** — element (g) may legitimately be 'unknown' early in an investigation; the report still must SAY so. Suppression: the field is populated with 'unknown' (true element) rather than null.

2. **No ransom payment in covered-incident report** — element (k) is conditional on a ransom payment; covered-incident reports without ransom have e_k=1 by default. Suppression: the SPL conditional handles this.

3. **Element-checkbox vs detailed-content** — a checkbox of `true` does not guarantee the content is meaningful; an IR Commander could check the box without writing real content. Suppression: maintain a content-quality review by General Counsel as part of the validator workflow.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `circia_22_54_12_exceptions` with per-draft grants requiring documented General Counsel approval and a 7-day maximum grant.
reviewer: CIRCIA Compliance SME Panel + IR Director
equipmentModels: Splunk Enterprise Security 7.x (Splunkbase 263, audit_evidence summary index)
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

- Splunk Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud.
- Splunk SOAR (Splunkbase 4413).
- KV Store `circia_submission_draft_lookup`.
- The two-stage SOAR `circia-portal-submit` (prefill+draft) and `circia-portal-transmit` (after-validation) playbooks.

## Step 1 — Define the submission-draft collection

```ini
[circia_submission_draft_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.submission_draft_id = string
field.incident_id = string
field.submission_type = string
field.element_a_entity_identification = bool
field.element_b_incident_description = bool
field.element_c_detection_determination_dates = bool
field.element_d_affected_systems = bool
field.element_e_impact = bool
field.element_f_vulnerabilities_exploited = bool
field.element_g_threat_actor = bool
field.element_h_security_defences = bool
field.element_i_mitigation_recovery = bool
field.element_j_point_of_contact = bool
field.element_k_ransom_payment_details = bool
field.draft_created_at = number
field.validator_signed_by = string
field.validator_signed_at = number
```

## Step 2 — SOAR prefill

`circia-portal-submit` writes a draft row with element flags set based on whether the corresponding ServiceNow SIR fields are populated:

- `e_a` true if covered-entity registry has the entity (UC-22.54.1).
- `e_b` true if SIR `description` is non-empty.
- `e_c` true if SIR `detected_at` and `reasonable_belief_at` (UC-22.54.10) are non-null.
- `e_d` true if SIR `affected_ci_list` is non-empty.
- `e_e` true if SIR impact-prong fields (UC-22.54.9) are populated.
- `e_f` true if SIR `vulnerabilities` field is non-empty.
- `e_g` true if SIR `threat_actor_identity` is non-empty (including the literal 'unknown').
- `e_h` true if SIR `security_defences_in_place` is non-empty.
- `e_i` true if SIR `mitigation_actions` is non-empty.
- `e_j` true if SIR `reporting_point_of_contact` is non-empty.
- `e_k` true for non-ransom covered-incident reports (conditional default), or if SIR `ransom_payment_details` is non-empty for ransom reports.

## Step 3 — Create the search and alert

```ini
[UC-22.54.12: CIRCIA Report-Content Template Validator]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -7d@d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.54.12,reg=CIRCIA,clause=CIRCIA-NPRM-report-content
action.notable.param.rule_title = CIRCIA submission draft incomplete vs NPRM § 226.8
action.notable.param.security_domain = audit
action.notable.param.severity = high
alert.severity = 4
```

## Step 4 — Validate deployment

**(a) Missing-element test**: write a draft with `element_h_security_defences=false`; UC-22.54.12 must flag `content_status=near_complete` with `missing_elements` listing `h_security_defences`.

**(b) Complete-draft test**: all 11 elements true; UC-22.54.12 must show `content_status=complete`.

**(c) Unknown threat-actor test**: SIR `threat_actor_identity=unknown`; the SOAR prefill sets `element_g_threat_actor=true` (because 'unknown' is a valid value); UC-22.54.12 records `complete`.

## Step 5 — Operationalize

**Runbook for `near_complete` or `incomplete`** (owner: IR Commander, severity P2):

1. Within 4 business hours: complete the missing elements; re-validate; only then trigger transmission.

## Step 6 — Troubleshooting

**Validator-bypassed transmission** — the SOAR `circia-portal-transmit` step must hard-gate on `content_status=complete`; if a transmission occurs without this gate, refactor the playbook.

**False-true flags** — an IR Commander may check elements true without writing meaningful content. Counter-control: General Counsel reviews drafts in a peer-review step before transmission.

**CIRCIA-specific failure modes**:

- **Element overlap with UC-22.54.9 four-prong**: NPRM elements (d), (e), (f), (g), (h), (i) are richer than the four-prong classification. The classification supports inclusion but the content fields must be drafted separately.
- **Unknown vs missing**: 'unknown' is a valid value for elements (g) and (f) early in an investigation; 'missing' (null) is not.
- **Submission-type-specific elements**: element (k) is conditional on submission_type=ransom; the SPL handles this.
grandmaExplanation: Before we send a report to the cyber agency, we tick off an eleven-point checklist — who we are, what happened, when, what was hit, the impact, the weakness, the attacker, our defences, what we did, our contact details, and any ransom paid. We don't send the report until all eleven boxes are ticked.
